The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The bioactive constituents of Mountain Dandelion include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Mountain Dandelion is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusate, and is used in traditional herbal medicine to support digestive health, alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, and promote wound healing.
